seed: batch-04 P5; criterial vs incidental features (Ped-4), grouping
passage: What makes an animal a mammal? Not where it lives: bats fly, whales swim, and platypuses lay eggs, yet all three are mammals. A shark swims like a whale but is not a mammal at all. The defining, criterial features are producing milk for the young and being warm-blooded. Habitat and even egg-laying are incidental — they vary across mammals and are not what makes the category.
